comment.body:
Every post follows from the other.
My base assumption is always that you read my analysis, understand it and re-read my references that I link in the post.
I repeat now something, that I saw you missed: spx/lty correlation is high (and it is even higher), so only bad news are bad news. No news and good news are both good news. Inflation is slowing down, they will say it, bcs thats a fact. 
But If you understand what I keep explaining for weeks, we are nowhere near to end the QT yet, we will when market will be lower after the OpEx. Trump wants sustained and steady growth. He wants to kill inflation.

comment.body:
Last year I spent my time posting two things: 1) Trumps gonna win (I tweeted about it in feb, way before anybody started to talk about 'trump trade' and stuff), 2) stagflation (I was laughed on, until it was officially said), and saying any ease would rip inflation. This is why I shorted the market after Sept cut, and won. 
That was too early for them. It was simply to boost the market in the short run, but market didn't bought it. 
Now, Trump wants to absolutely fight the stagflation (on papers), to give room for a real, actual, sustained QE. Clear?

comment.body:
In my calculations, I’m seeing the 21D SPY/TNX Beta hitting the highest value in the last 5 years (100th percentile). Additionally, the 10D SPY/TLT correlation is at an extremely negative level. So, if the Fed today, as ALMA suggests, acknowledges the latest data (i.e., inflation is slowing down, the economy has slightly decelerated due to tariffs, etc.) and leans slightly dovish, then the market will, as ALMA puts it, recognize this as good news. A mildly dovish Fed would generally have a negative impact on yields and a positive effect on SPY and TLT, leading to a reversal of my extreme values (i.e., reversal of the extremely high 21D SPY/TNX Beta and reversal of the extremely negative 10D SPY/TLT correlation).
Please correct me if I’m misinterpreting anything. But this would also align with ALMA’s bias mentioned above. Plus, there are some key points in the Weekly Post worth noting:
"Meanwhile, certain circles on the Street are saying we will see positive signals from the FED this week."
"Spot/vol beta has been trending downward! This is a major hidden strength in the current momentum."
"Both I and my algo have noticed that most funds have switched to high-frequency mean reversion trading."

comment.body: To clear up some of the confusion: Quarterly Opex is the OPEX in the third week of March, June, Sep, Dec. Not the end of the quarter. It's important because the quarterly option contracts are the most liquid and usually have the highest open interest. That's also when the quarterly futures (ES, NQ, etc) expire
comment.raw_body: To clear up some of the confusion: Quarterly Opex is the OPEX in the third week of March, June, Sep, Dec. Not the end of the quarter. It's important because the quarterly option contracts are the most liquid and usually have the highest open interest. That's also when the quarterly futures (ES, NQ, etc) expire

comment.body: And since the daily, weekly, monthly and quarterly options expire on this day, it's called Quad Witching as stated above
